name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - San Antonio, Tx
description_short: NACA offers character-based mortgages with no down payment, no closing costs, and no mortgage insurance, serving low-to-moderate income homebuyers regardless of credit score.
description_long:
The Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America (NACA) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union to challenge predatory lending practices and provide affordable homeownership to historically underserved communities. What began as a housing trust fund initiative has evolved into a national organization that has served 3 million people and helped over 500,000 homebuyers achieve homeownership. NACA operates as a mission-driven nonprofit focused on closing the racial wealth disparity gap through character-based lending rather than credit-score-based decisions.

NACA's primary offering is their Best in America Mortgage program, which features no down payment requirements, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance premiums, and interest rates that do not factor in credit scores. As of April 2026, they offer 30-year fixed rates at 5.625%, 20-year at 5.125%, and 15-year at 5%. The organization also manages a Member Portal where applicants can provide information, upload documents, and check their application status. NACA maintains $20 billion in mortgage commitments and provides HUD housing counseling services (representing 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally). Beyond mortgages, they advocate against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors through their NACtivist volunteer network.

What distinguishes NACA is their character-based underwriting model that explicitly does not penalize applicants for poor credit histories. They evaluate borrowers' circumstances holistically rather than relying solely on credit scores, making them unique among traditional mortgage lenders. Their Achieve the Dream Events (held in cities like Hartford, Little Rock, Milwaukee, and Fort Myers) provide in-person access to their services and qualification process. As a nonprofit with 36 years of community advocacy experience, NACA combines mortgage lending with active campaigns against predatory lending practices and corporate real estate consolidation.

However, prospective borrowers should understand that NACA requires participation in their qualification process, which involves attending multi-day Achieve the Dream Events or engaging with their counseling program. While their rates are competitive and their down payment/closing cost elimination is valuable, the character-based underwriting model and nonprofit structure mean they operate differently from conventional lenders. Their focus on underserved and low-to-moderate income communities is intentional, and their rates reflect market conditions as of specific dates. Applicants should verify current offerings and interest rates directly with NACA.
pros: No down payment required for qualified borrowers
No closing costs or fees, eliminating typical mortgage expenses
No mortgage insurance (PMI) required regardless of down payment
Character-based underwriting that does not consider credit score
Below-market fixed rates (5.625% for 30-year as of April 2026)
Provides HUD-approved housing counseling (30% of national HUD counseling)
Extensive portfolio: $20 billion in mortgage commitments and 75,000+ NACA mortgages originated
cons: Requires participation in Achieve the Dream Events or lengthy qualification process, not a streamlined online application
Limited geographic reach at any given time; events held only in select cities on specific dates
No mention of alternative lending programs (FHA, VA, jumbo mortgages, refinancing options)
As a nonprofit with advocacy mission, may have longer processing times than conventional lenders
Character-based model, while beneficial for some, provides limited information on actual approval rates or typical borrower profiles
best_for: Low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers with poor or no credit history
Minority borrowers and communities historically excluded from conventional lending
Buyers in target event cities (Hartford, Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, St. Louis, Chicago) who can attend multi-day qualification events
Homebuyers seeking to avoid mortgage insurance and closing costs as primary financial barriers
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers with limited credit history or poor credit scores who can participate in their qualification events and seek mortgages with no down payment and no closing costs. The main caveat is that their model requires active participation in multi-day Achieve the Dream Events held in specific cities on set dates, making accessibility dependent on geography and schedule flexibility.
services: Member portal for document submission and status tracking
Achieve the Dream Events (in-person qualification workshops)
Mortgage rate locks and fixed-rate loan options (15, 20, 30-year terms)
NACtivist volunteer coordination and advocacy programs
Real estate education and homeownership preparation
Partnership programs with lenders, servicers, and real estate professionals
Podcast content on affordable housing and economic justice
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1603 Babcock Rd Suite 200, San Antonio, TX 78229
